---
title: ""
page-layout: full
---

::: {.hero-section}
# Institutional Alpha
### S&P 500 Sector-Neutral Walk-Forward
<p>Trade the open. Let factors decide.</p>
:::

<h2 class="section-title">Performance Metrics</h2>

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.metric-card}
::: {.metric-icon}
<i class="fas fa-chart-line"></i>
:::
::: {.metric-value}
78.13%
:::
::: {.metric-label}
Total Return
:::
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.metric-card}
::: {.metric-icon}
<i class="fas fa-gem"></i>
:::
::: {.metric-value}
0.92
:::
::: {.metric-label}
Sharpe Ratio
:::
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.metric-card}
::: {.metric-icon}
<i class="fas fa-percentage"></i>
:::
::: {.metric-value}
14.36%
:::
::: {.metric-label}
Annualized Return
:::
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.metric-card}
::: {.metric-icon}
<i class="fas fa-shield-alt"></i>
:::
::: {.metric-value}
-16.36%
:::
::: {.metric-label}
Max Drawdown
:::
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.metric-card}
::: {.metric-icon}
<i class="fas fa-bullseye"></i>
:::
::: {.metric-value}
53.78%
:::
::: {.metric-label}
Daily Hit Rate
:::
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.metric-card}
::: {.metric-icon}
<i class="fas fa-check-circle"></i>
:::
::: {.metric-value}
51.94%
:::
::: {.metric-label}
Trade Success Rate
:::
:::
:::

:::

<h2 class="section-title">Visual Equity Curve</h2>

::: {.chart-container}
![](output/cumulative_returns.png){width="100%"}
:::

::: {.analysis-box}
### Performance Insight
The cumulative equity curve demonstrates consistent outperformance against the SPY benchmark (Red). The strategy effectively captures alpha during both trending and sideways markets, ending with a substantial **78.13% total return** compared to the benchmark's significantly lower growth over the same 2022-2026 horizon.
:::

<h2 class="section-title">Risk Exposure Analysis</h2>

::: {.chart-container}
![](output/drawdown.png){width="100%"}
:::

::: {.analysis-box}
### Risk Management Insight
Our **-16.36% Max Drawdown** profile highlights the robustness of the sector-neutral constraint. While the broad market experienced severe corrections exceeding -25% during this period, our strategy's diversified factor exposure and strict stop-loss limits preserved capital and enabled rapid recoveries.
:::

<h2 class="section-title">Period-by-Period Performance</h2>

::: {.data-box}
::: {.scroll-table}
```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
import pandas as pd
folds = pd.read_csv('output/walk_forward_folds.csv')
folds = folds.rename(columns={
    'fold': 'Test Period',
    'strategy_cumulative_return': 'Return',
    'strategy_sharpe': 'Sharpe',
    'excess_cumulative_return': 'Excess Return',
    'average_number_of_longs': 'N Stocks'
})
for col in ['Return', 'Excess Return']:
    folds[col] = folds[col].apply(lambda x: f"{x*100:.2f}%")
folds['Sharpe'] = folds['Sharpe'].apply(lambda x: f"{x:.2f}")
folds['N Stocks'] = folds['N Stocks'].apply(lambda x: f"{int(x)}")
print(folds[['Test Period', 'Return', 'Sharpe', 'Excess Return', 'N Stocks']].to_markdown(index=False))
```
:::
:::

::: {.analysis-box}
### Rolling Fold Analysis
The walk-forward folds confirm the strategy's adaptability. The **N Stocks** column shows a consistent selection size (~40-44 stocks), ensuring deep diversification. The **Excess Return** column proves that the alpha signal remained persistent across almost all 6-month out-of-sample periods.
:::

<h2 class="section-title">Factor Efficiency Summary</h2>

::: {.data-box}
::: {.scroll-table}
```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
import pandas as pd
ic = pd.read_csv('output/ic_summary.csv')
name_map = {
    'momentum_252_21_ic': '12M Momentum',
    'proximity_52w_high_ic': '52W High Proximity',
    'trend_quality_126_ic': '6M Trend Quality',
    'momentum_60_5_ic': '3M Momentum',
    'combined_score_ic': 'Multi-Factor Composite'
}
ic['Factor Name'] = ic['factor'].map(name_map)
ic['Mean IC'] = ic['mean_ic'].apply(lambda x: f"{x:.4f}")
ic['T-Stat'] = ic['ic_t_stat'].apply(lambda x: f"{x:.2f}")
ic['Positive Ratio'] = ic['positive_ic_ratio'].apply(lambda x: f"{x*100:.1f}%")
print(ic[['Factor Name', 'Mean IC', 'T-Stat', 'Positive Ratio']].to_markdown(index=False))
```
:::
:::

::: {.analysis-box}
### Statistical Significance
The **12M Momentum** factor is our strongest alpha contributor with a T-Stat of 2.50, clearing the standard hurdle for statistical significance. The **Multi-Factor Composite** maintains a high positive IC ratio, ensuring that when individual factors decay, the composite signal remains stable.
:::

<h2 class="section-title">Dynamic Weight Evolution</h2>

::: {.data-box}
::: {.scroll-table}
```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
import pandas as pd
weights = pd.read_csv('output/walk_forward_factor_weights.csv')
weights['Period'] = weights['fold'].apply(lambda x: x.split(' to ')[0])
weights = weights.rename(columns={
    'weight_momentum_252_21': 'W_12M_Mom',
    'weight_proximity_52w_high': 'W_52W_High',
    'weight_trend_quality_126': 'W_6M_Trend',
    'weight_momentum_60_5': 'W_3M_Mom'
})
cols = ['Period', 'W_12M_Mom', 'W_52W_High', 'W_6M_Trend', 'W_3M_Mom']
for col in cols[1:]:
    weights[col] = weights[col].apply(lambda x: f"{x:.2f}")
print(weights[cols].to_markdown(index=False))
```
:::
:::

::: {.analysis-box}
### Optimization Dynamics
Factor weights are not static. Our walk-forward engine automatically pivots capital towards the best-performing signals. For instance, **12M Momentum** weight increased during trending periods, while **6M Trend Quality** provided stability during regime transitions.
:::

<h2 class="section-title">Detailed Trade Records</h2>

::: {.data-box}
::: {.scroll-table}
```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
import pandas as pd
df = pd.read_csv('blotter_preview.csv')
df['Return %'] = df['return_pct'].apply(lambda x: f"{x*100:.2f}%")
cols = ['signal_date', 'ticker', 'sector', 'entry_price', 'exit_price', 'Return %', 'fate']
print(df[cols].to_markdown(index=False))
```
:::
:::

::: {.analysis-box}
### Trade Execution Analysis
The trade blotter reveals our **"Trade the Open"** philosophy. Positions are entered and exited with minimal market impact. The **Fate** column confirms that the 3.0% stop-loss effectively "cuts tails," preventing outliers from damaging the portfolio.
:::

<h2 class="section-title">Account Ledger</h2>

::: {.data-box}
::: {.scroll-table}
```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
import pandas as pd
df = pd.read_csv('ledger_preview.csv')
df['Return %'] = df['daily_return'].apply(lambda x: f"{x*100:.2f}%")
df['Portfolio Value'] = df['portfolio_value'].apply(lambda x: f"${x:,.2f}")
cols = ['date', 'Portfolio Value', 'daily_pnl', 'Return %']
print(df[cols].to_markdown(index=False))
```
:::
:::

::: {.analysis-box}
### Capital Growth Analysis
The daily ledger samples show the steady appreciation of the **$100,000 initial capital**. The low volatility in daily PnL reflects the benefit of the sector-neutral framework, which focuses on relative stock performance rather than market direction.
:::

<h2 class="section-title">Methodology</h2>

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.method-card}
### Entry Logic
The strategy employs a **Sector-Neutral** framework to focus on idiosyncratic alpha. 

*   **Universe**: S&P 500 constituents.
*   **Optimization**: 3-year rolling training window.
*   **Selection**: Daily rebalancing into top decile names.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.method-card}
### Risk Management
Strict risk controls ensure long-term strategy survival.

*   **Exit**: Daily rotation to fresh signals.
*   **Stop-Loss**: 3.0% hard intra-day exit.
*   **Audit**: 100% fate tracking for all trades.
:::
:::

:::

::: {.footer}
Quant Alpha Strategy | Fintech 533 Mini-Capstone | 2026
:::
